epic.name: Enhanced Alert Deduplication & Grouping Rules
epic.objective: Enhance alert deduplication for multi-source environments and implement alert grouping rules that combine related alerts into a single incident based on configurable criteria (service, time window, labels)
epic.definition_of_done: Related alerts are automatically grouped into a single incident based on configurable rules. Cross-source deduplication handles alerts from multiple monitoring tools for the same underlying issue. Grouped alerts are visible on the incident detail page.
epic.notes: v0.1 deduplication uses (source, external_id) which only handles exact duplicates from the same source. v0.3 needs: (1) grouping rules that link related-but-different alerts to one incident, and (2) cross-source awareness so an alert from Prometheus and Grafana about the same service don't create two incidents.

tasks.name: Design alert grouping rules data model
tasks.description: Create the data model for alert grouping rules that define when multiple alerts should be combined into a single incident
acceptance_criteria: GroupingRule model defined: id, name, description, enabled, priority, match_labels (JSONB), time_window (duration), created_at, updated_at
match_labels supports exact match and regex match on alert labels (e.g., {service: 'api-*', env: 'production'})
time_window defines the window in which matching alerts are grouped (default: 5 minutes)
Database migration created: 000007_create_grouping_rules_table
GroupingRuleRepository interface with CRUD operations
Seed default grouping rule: group by alertname label within 5-minute window
tasks.implementation_notes: Grouping rules work similarly to Alertmanager's group_by config but stored in DB for runtime configuration. Priority determines evaluation order when multiple rules match.

tasks.name: Implement alert grouping engine
tasks.description: Build the core grouping engine that evaluates incoming alerts against grouping rules and determines whether to create a new incident or link to an existing one
acceptance_criteria: GroupingEngine service created with EvaluateAlert(alert NormalizedAlert) (*Incident, bool, error)
Returns existing incident if alert matches an open incident's grouping criteria
Returns nil if no matching group found (caller should create new incident)
Matching logic: check each enabled rule in priority order
For each rule: match labels against alert labels using exact/regex matching
If labels match: look for open incident created within rule's time_window with same grouping key
Grouping key derived from matched label values (e.g., 'service=api-gateway,env=prod')
Performance: grouping evaluation completes in <10ms for 100 rules
Thread-safe: concurrent webhook calls don't create duplicate incidents for the same group
tasks.implementation_notes: The grouping engine sits between alert normalization and incident creation in the pipeline. Use a database-level advisory lock on the grouping key to prevent race conditions. Cache active grouping rules with short TTL.

tasks.name: Integrate grouping engine into alert processing pipeline
tasks.description: Wire the grouping engine into ProcessNormalizedAlerts() so that incoming alerts are evaluated against grouping rules before incident creation
acceptance_criteria: ProcessNormalizedAlerts() calls groupingEngine.EvaluateAlert() before creating incident
If grouping returns existing incident: link alert to that incident instead of creating new
Timeline entry created when alert grouped: type=alert_linked, content includes grouping_rule_name
If no grouping match: existing behavior (create new incident if severity warrants)
Grouped alerts appear in incident detail page under 'Linked Alerts' section
ProcessingResult updated with grouped_count in addition to created/updated
Grouping disabled gracefully if no rules configured (falls through to existing behavior)
tasks.implementation_notes: Must maintain backwards compatibility. With zero grouping rules configured, the system should behave identically to v0.2.

tasks.name: Implement cross-source alert correlation
tasks.description: Add the ability to correlate alerts from different sources that refer to the same underlying issue, preventing duplicate incidents
acceptance_criteria: Correlation based on configurable label matching across sources (e.g., host, service, instance)
Correlation labels configurable in grouping rules: cross_source_labels field
When alert from source A matches open incident from source B on correlation labels → link to same incident
Timeline entry: type=cross_source_correlation, content includes both source names
Cross-source correlation is opt-in (not enabled by default to avoid false positives)
Example: Prometheus alert (host=web-01) and CloudWatch alarm (InstanceId=i-xxx, tag:Name=web-01) correlated via host/Name mapping
tasks.implementation_notes: Cross-source correlation is inherently fuzzy because different monitoring tools use different label names for the same concept. Start with exact label value matching after optional label name remapping.

tasks.name: Grouping rules CRUD API
tasks.description: Create REST API endpoints for managing alert grouping rules
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/v1/grouping-rules → list all grouping rules
GET /api/v1/grouping-rules/:id → get single rule
POST /api/v1/grouping-rules → create rule with validation
PATCH /api/v1/grouping-rules/:id → update rule
DELETE /api/v1/grouping-rules/:id → delete rule (soft delete)
Validation: name required, time_window > 0, match_labels valid JSON
Audit: created_at, updated_at tracked
Cache invalidation on create/update/delete
tasks.implementation_notes: Standard CRUD following existing patterns in handlers/ and repository/. Use the same handler/service/repository layering as incidents.

tasks.name: Grouped alerts UI on incident detail page
tasks.description: Display linked/grouped alerts on the incident detail page and add a grouping rules management page
acceptance_criteria: Incident detail page shows 'Linked Alerts' section with all grouped alerts
Each alert shows: source icon, title, severity badge, status, received_at
Alerts sorted by received_at (newest first)
Click on alert expands to show labels and raw payload preview
Source icons differentiated: Prometheus (flame), Grafana (eye), CloudWatch (AWS logo), Generic (webhook icon)
Settings page: grouping rules list with enable/disable toggle
Settings page: create/edit grouping rule form
tasks.implementation_notes: API endpoint GET /api/v1/incidents/:id/alerts already exists from v0.1. Focus on the UI display. Source icons can be simple SVG or emoji initially.

tasks.name: Integration tests for grouping and cross-source correlation
tasks.description: Comprehensive tests for alert grouping rules engine and cross-source correlation
acceptance_criteria: Test: two alerts with same label within time window → grouped into one incident
Test: two alerts with same label outside time window → two separate incidents
Test: alert matches no grouping rule → creates new incident (existing behavior)
Test: cross-source correlation links Prometheus + Grafana alerts to same incident
Test: grouping rule disabled → alerts not grouped by that rule
Test: concurrent alerts within same group → no duplicate incidents (race condition test)
Test: grouping rules CRUD API endpoints
All tests pass without external dependencies
tasks.implementation_notes: Concurrency test is critical — use Go's testing race detector and parallel goroutines to verify advisory lock behavior.
